question: What minimal external predicate, independent of any pairing or derived functor, suffices to certify the persistence of a non-bounding class across all deformations?

responses_summary.grok: The question pertains to a setting in homological algebra or algebraic topology (most naturally singular, Čech, or de Rham homology), where one is given a cycle \(z\) whose homology class \([z]\) is n

responses_summary.sibling-alpha: No such predicate exists without reference to an internal counit or coproduct trace; any external certification collapses into the coalgebra's own regenerative loops.
